name: Dakota Bus Svc Inc
principals: Part of Dakota Trailways / DBS family of companies; Phyllis Charles listed as local manager
districts_or_customers: Colorado Front Range school/charter customers; operates as DBS/Grace Coach Lines from Lafayette, CO
recent_news: DBS/Grace Coach Lines markets as a third-generation family-owned operator (Dakota Trailways heritage since 1957); no recent M&A signal found
fit_notes: Small CO operator with school/charter mix and family/third-gen ownership; modest size but Front Range Denver-metro footprint could be a tuck-in for a Summit Colorado build, though school exposure looks limited vs. charter.

name: M & J Bus Inc
principals: Michael C. Beebe (President/Founder), Karen A. Beebe (Co-founder), Michael John 'MJ' Beebe (next-gen)
districts_or_customers: ~23-28 CT towns including Old Saybrook, Clinton, Farmington, Colchester, Windham, Coventry, Lyme-Old Lyme (Region 18), North Stonington, Westbrook, Stafford, Mansfield, Suffield, plus E.O. Smith HS athletics
recent_news: Profiled in 2024 as one of CT's heavyweight school transportation operators; founder still active with son in management (succession dynamic)
fit_notes: Highly attractive: 50-yr family-owned CT platform with ~400 buses/175 vans, 23+ municipal contracts, founder in 70s+ with one son in business — classic succession-driven tuck-in/regional platform candidate for Summit in CT.

name: Caldwell Transportation Co Inc
principals: Karen Azman (listed manager); locally owned/operated per company materials
districts_or_customers: Mountain Home School District (since 2013), plus Treasure Valley districts via Caldwell/Boise/Middleton hubs; 4,000+ students/day
recent_news: Self-described locally owned, 75+ years in business; no recent M&A signal found
fit_notes: Decades-old, locally owned Idaho platform with multi-hub Treasure Valley footprint (Caldwell/Boise/Middleton/Mountain Home) and ~140 buses — strong tuck-in candidate to seed Summit's Mountain West/Idaho presence.

name: Ottawa Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Olathe, KS-based; operates as Crossroad Tours — primarily charter/tour business, limited evidence of contracted school district routes
recent_news: Long-standing (~87 years per D&B) Kansas charter operator under Crossroad Tours brand; no M&A signals
fit_notes: Likely off-thesis: appears predominantly a motorcoach/charter operator (Crossroad Tours) rather than a contracted yellow-bus district provider; small (~21 units). Confirm school exposure before pursuing — otherwise pass.

name: North America Central Sch Bus
owner_or_parent: H.I.G. Capital (H.I.G. Infrastructure, acquired Aug 2021 from Goldner Hawn)
districts_or_customers: ~90 school district customers across 9 states (primarily Midwest); ~150,000 students/day
recent_news: August 2021: H.I.G. Infrastructure acquired NACSB from Goldner Hawn; prior Gryphon Junior Capital investment; mgmt under CEO Steve Hemmerlein retained for geographic expansion + EV transition
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — NACSB is itself a PE-owned platform (~3,000 buses, top-5 US student transport operator) under H.I.G. Infrastructure. Competitor/peer to Summit, not acquirable at the tuck-in scale. Exclude from pipeline.
